content.text:
**Step 1 — Orientation.**

Pebble is a LevelDB/RocksDB-inspired embedded key-value store (the storage engine under CockroachDB). The write batch handle is `pebble.Batch`, defined at `batch.go:240` (embedding `batchInternal`, which holds the `data []byte` repr). It is already pooled at the repr level via `batchPool` (`batch.go:482`) / `indexedBatchPool` (`batch.go:493`), allocated in `newBatch` (`batch.go:499`).

Direct holders (the class the team can already enumerate — a field literally typed `*Batch`/`Batch`, or a type transitively containing one):
- `indexedBatch` — `batch.go:488` (`batch Batch`)
- `batchIter` — `batch.go:1761` (`batch *Batch`)
- `iteratorBatchState.batch *Batch` — `iterator.go:393`, embedded into `Iterator.batch` (`iterator.go:295`), which also embeds `pointIter batchIter` (`iterator.go:405`)
- the commit pipeline queue slots in `commit.go`

Now the actual work.

content.text:
**Confirmed #1 — WAL failover record queue (two struct sites in one file):**

| # | struct (file:line) | abstraction stored | concrete carrier | injection site | evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1a | `wal.recordQueueEntry.refCount` — `wal/failover_writer.go:25` (also holds `p []byte` = the batch's repr) | `wal.RefCount` interface (`wal/wal.go:434`) | `*pebble.Batch` (implements `Ref` at `batch.go:291`) | `db.go:908` and `db.go:950`: `d.mu.log.writer.WriteRecord(repr, ..., b)` — `b` is the live `*Batch`, `repr` is `b.data` | confirmed at source |
| 1b | `wal.poppedEntry.refCount` — `wal/failover_writer.go:31` | `wal.RefCount` | same `*Batch`, copied out of the queue in `recordQueue.pop` (`wal/failover_writer.go:259`), unref'd at `:272-273` | `wal/failover_writer.go:259` | confirmed at source |

This is the sharpest one: the failover writer retains the batch's *byte slice* and only releases it via `Unref` after the record is durably synced — potentially long after the caller's `Apply` returned.

**Confirmed #5–#8 — range-key stack / masking:**

| # | struct (file:line) | abstraction | concrete carrier | injection site | evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | `keyspanimpl.mergingIterLevel.iter` — `internal/keyspan/keyspanimpl/merging_iter.go:237` | `keyspan.FragmentIterator` | the batch's range-key fragment iter `&i.batch.rangeKeyIter` (a `keyspan.Iter` over spans whose key bytes alias `batch.data`) | `range_keys.go:44` → `UserIteratorConfig.AddLevel` (`internal/rangekeystack/user_iterator.go:117-119`) → `MergingIter.AddLevel` (`.../merging_iter.go:335-339`) | confirmed at source |
| 6 | `keyspan.BoundedIter.iter` — `internal/keyspan/bounded.go:58` | `keyspan.FragmentIterator` | wraps the `MergingIter` above (`UserIteratorConfig.biter`, `internal/rangekeystack/user_iterator.go:59`) — transitively the batch level | `UserIteratorConfig.Init`, `internal/rangekeystack/user_iterator.go:81+` | confirmed at source |
| 7 | `keyspan.DefragmentingIter.iter` — `internal/keyspan/defragment.go:136` | `keyspan.FragmentIterator` | wraps the `BoundedIter` above (`UserIteratorConfig.diter`, `:60`) | same `Init` | confirmed at source |
| 8 | `iteratorRangeKeyState.rangeKeyIter` — `iterator.go:419` | `keyspan.FragmentIterator` | the whole stack above, rooted at the batch's range-key iter; optionally wrapped again by `keyspan.InjectLogging` (`range_keys.go:30`) | `range_keys.go:22` | confirmed at source |

Range-key **masking** additionally parks batch-backed bytes in `rangeKeyMasking.maskSpan *keyspan.Span` / `maskActiveSuffix []byte` (`range_keys.go:239-243`) — the span's key bytes alias `batch.data` when the covering range key came from the batch level; `rangeKeyMasking` itself is installed as the `keyspan.SpanMask` interface in `InterleavingIterOpts.Mask`.

**Confirmed #9 — the memtable/flushable queue (the big one):**

| # | struct (file:line) | abstraction | concrete carrier | injection site | evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 9 | `flushableEntry` embeds the `flushable` interface — `flushable.go:71` (interface at `flushable.go:22`); entries live in `d.mu.mem.queue` | `flushable` interface | `*flushableBatch`, whose `data []byte` (`batch.go:1977`) **is** `batch.data` — same backing array, no copy (`batch.go:2009`) | `db.go:832` (`batch.flushable, err = newFlushableBatch(batch, ...)`), consumed into the queue at `db.go:2821-2826`; also `recovery.go:620` | confirmed at source |

The flushable queue is read by the **flush goroutine**, not the writer — a batch whose repr is aliased here outlives the caller's `Apply` entirely.
